Skills.Name: Allegoria
Skills.Description:
<color=#FFD780FF>Normal Attack</color>
Proclaims a decree in the primordial tongue and performs up to 3 attacks that deal <color=#FF9999FF>Pyro DMG</color>.

<color=#FFD780FF>Charged Attack</color>
Consumes a certain amount of Stamina to deal <color=#FF9999FF>AoE Pyro DMG</color> to opponents in front of her after a short casting time.

<color=#FFD780FF>Plunging Attack</color>
Nicole plunges towards the ground from mid-air, damaging all opponents in her path. Also deals <color=#FF9999FF>AoE Pyro DMG</color> upon impact with the ground.

Descriptions: Grace of Kenosis ATK Bonus Ratio|{param5:F2P} ATK
Grace of Kenosis Max ATK Bonus|{param6:F1}

Skills.Name: Revelation: Uncreated Light
Skills.Description:
Recreates a fleeting glimpse of an age long past, when the angels acted on the authority of the heavens, bestowing the {LINK#N11310001}<color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color>{/LINK}{LINK#N11310002}{/LINK} effect on nearby party members. This increases their ATK and deals <color=#FF9999FF>AoE Pyro DMG</color>, as well as creates a Shield of Blazing Light.
The Shield of Blazing Light's DMG Absorption is based on Nicole's ATK, and it will absorb <color=#FF9999FF>Pyro DMG</color> at 250% effectiveness.

SpecialSkills.Name: Witch's Eve Rite: Light in the Darkness
SpecialSkills.Description:
After completing <color=#FFD780FF>Witch's Homework: The Role of a Guide...?</color>, Nicole becomes a <color=#FFD780FF>Hexerei</color> character. When the party includes at least 2 Hexerei characters, you gain the <color=#FFD780FF>Hexerei: Secret Rite</color> effect, which enhances Hexerei characters.

<color=#FFD780FF>Hexerei: Secret Rite</color>
Increases the DMG dealt by Hexerei characters' <color=#FFD780FF>{LINK#S11315}Arcane Projections{/LINK}</color> by 300% of Nicole's ATK.

Descriptions: Arcane Projection DMG|{param2:F1P} Corresponding Character's ATK

EnergySkill.Name: Revelation: Ladder of Divine Ascent
EnergySkill.Description:
As Nicole weaves the path of anchored fate with golden threads of light, she deals <color=#FF9999FF>AoE Pyro DMG</color> and switches to Silent Contemplation mode.
In this mode, when nearby active party members hit an opponent, an Arcane Projection is summoned to perform a Coordinated Attack against the opponent, dealing AoE Elemental DMG of the character's corresponding Elemental Type. This DMG scales based on the corresponding character's ATK and will be considered as having been dealt by them.
This effect can occur once every 3s, and can be triggered up to 4 times each time Nicole switches to Silent Contemplation mode.

It is worth noting that Hexerei characters' Arcane Projections appear to differ somehow in appearance from the others...

Inherents.Name: Methexis
Inherents.Description:
For 20s after unleashing the Elemental Skill <color=#FFD780FF>{LINK#S11312}Revelation: Uncreated Light{/LINK}</color>, Nicole will continuously "observe" the other current active party members. After the observed character has remained on the field for 3s, their {LINK#N11310001}<color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color>{/LINK} is upgraded to {LINK#N11310002}<color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color>{/LINK}, increasing their ATK by an additional 300. If the observed character is a Hexerei character, <color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color> is upgraded to <color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color> straight away.
When characters other than Nicole who have <color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color> leave the field, their effect reverts to <color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color>.

Inherents.Name: Philokalia
Inherents.Description: When Elemental DMG from nearby active party members hits an opponent, Nicole's own {LINK#N11310001}<color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color>{/LINK} effect is upgraded to {LINK#N11310002}<color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color>{/LINK} for 8s.

Inherents.Name: Nepsis
Inherents.Description:
When you are in Teyvat and not in combat, Nicole's Charged Attack becomes "Emissarial Guidance" if you have already obtained the current region's Treasure Compass. Emissarial Guidance: Calls upon nearby Seelie to show the way, triggering an effect similar to the region's Treasure Compass.
This effect has a cooldown time of 5 seconds and does not work in Domains, Trounce Domains, and the Spiral Abyss.

Talents.Name: "Do Not Be Afraid, Child Who Is Loved"
Talents.Description:
When the current active character of your own in the party hits an opponent, an additional special "Arcane Projection: Unity" will be summoned to perform a Coordinated Attack against the opponent, dealing AoE Elemental DMG of the character's corresponding Elemental Type at 600% of their ATK. This DMG will be considered as having been dealt by the corresponding character.
This effect can trigger once every 6s.

Talents.Name: "I Will Guide You and Show You the Path You Should Tread"
Talents.Description:
Enhances the effects of the Elemental Skill <color=#FFD780FF>{LINK#S11312}Revelation: Uncreated Light{/LINK}</color>: {LINK#N11310001}<color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color>{/LINK} increases ATK by an additional 300 (which does not count toward this effect's ATK Bonus limit), while {LINK#N11310002}<color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color>{/LINK} decreases nearby opponents' Elemental RES based on the corresponding character's Elemental Type by 25%. This effect does not stack for the same Element.
Additionally, when Nicole unleashes her Elemental Skill <color=#FFD780FF>Revelation: Uncreated Light</color>, nearby active party members will also gain a Shield of Blazing Light.

Talents.Name: "A Lamp by Your Side, A Light to Shine the Way"
Talents.Description:
Increases the Level of <color=#FFD780FF>{LINK#S11312}Revelation: Uncreated Light{/LINK}</color> by 3.
Maximum upgrade level is 15.

Talents.Name: "Whether Left or Right, No Matter Which Way You Turn"
Talents.Description:
When nearby party members have their {LINK#N11310001}<color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color>{/LINK} upgraded to {LINK#N11310002}<color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color>{/LINK}, Nicole also bestows upon them the Pathfinder's Blessing effect for 20s. Each character can gain this effect once every 16s.
The Normal Attack, Charged Attack, Plunging Attack, Elemental Skill, and Elemental Burst DMG of characters with Pathfinder's Blessing is increased by 70% of Nicole's ATK. This effect will be removed once it has taken effect 8 times or when its duration is over.
When multiple opponents are hit at once, instances of Pathfinder's Blessing are consumed equal to the number of opponents hit. Each character's Pathfinder's Blessing is counted independently.

Talents.Name: "You Will Hear My Voice Beside You"
Talents.Description:
Increases the Level of <color=#FFD780FF>{LINK#S11315}Revelation: Ladder of Divine Ascent{/LINK}</color> by 3.
Maximum upgrade level is 15.

Talents.Name: "This Is the Path, Walk It Without Delay"
Talents.Description:
When Nicole's {LINK#N11310001}<color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color>{/LINK} is upgraded to {LINK#N11310002}<color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color>{/LINK}, all nearby party members' <color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color> will also be upgraded to <color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color>. Once upgraded, <color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color> will no longer revert to <color=#FFD780FF>Grace of Kenosis</color>.
Additionally, DMG dealt by characters with <color=#FFD780FF>Guidance of Theosis</color> will ignore 40% of opponents' DEF.

Fetters.Title: Hello
Fetters.Context: Oh, should I introduce myself again? Hm, looks like no interference from Alice's self-introduction magic this time, so I suppose I'll just go ahead... Ahem, I'm Nicole. As you can see, I'm— Ah, again! Guess I spoke too soon...

Fetters.Title: Chat: Novels
Fetters.Context: Hm... Not the best idea I've ever had.

Fetters.Title: Chat: Statue
Fetters.Context: Mm... Metaphors speak for themselves.

Fetters.Title: Chat: Silence
Fetters.Context: What am I now, you ask? Just a "human" who's lost her voice.

Fetters.Title: After the Rain
Fetters.Context: In truth, it's thanks to the Seelie that there's always treasure at the end of the rainbow.

Fetters.Title: Good Morning
Fetters.Context: Good morning.

Fetters.Title: Good Afternoon
Fetters.Context: Good afternoon.

Fetters.Title: Good Evening
Fetters.Context: Nighttime is when inspiration strikes the most, so it was also the most common time for people of the past to encounter angels. I suppose they're the ones who are largely to blame for why we turned into a bunch of night owls. Although, that's not quite accurate. It's not like we had biological clocks or sleep cycles. What we did was more akin to... regular functional maintenance. So, I suppose it was just a matter of avoiding the evening hours...

Fetters.Title: Good Night
Fetters.Context: Heading to bed already? Hehe, good night, then.

Fetters.Title: About Nicole: Popular Works
Fetters.Context: As long as an author still lives, the most they can hope for is to create something popular. People are an amalgamation of the present, after all. Just as it's impossible for an Akademiya scholar to have twenty years of knowledge at the beginning of their studies, a modern author cannot hope to write a classic in their own time. In other words, all one can do is simply write what is interesting. Anyway, I'm not the one doing the writing, so I feel little pressure in that regard.

Fetters.Title: About Nicole: Allegories
Fetters.Context: In the past, angels often relied on allegory as a means of communication. Why? Because as society changes, allegories lose their effectiveness, so more often than not, the key to interpreting them is lost forever... and I must say, thank goodness for that. Sometimes, the people of the past would obsess over the strangest questions — the kinds we couldn't just answer or ignore. But toss a cryptic allegory at them and... Problem solved. All that to say, if an angel starts speaking in metaphors and allegories, you might be asking the wrong question. My sculptures are a perfect example of this tactic. The little squirrel is actually a metaphor for humanity.

Fetters.Title: About Us: Ghostwriting
Fetters.Context: Lately, it's been getting harder and harder to get Mr. Nine to listen to me. Ever since regaining her intellect, all she does is argue with me about plot points and writing choices. You're quite knowledgeable. Any interest in becoming my new ghostwriter?

Fetters.Title: About Us: Adventuring
Fetters.Context: In terms of us working together, I suppose going on adventures is the safest option. If I were to go through event after event since the beginning of time, things could get dangerous. You have my full trust, of course, but I'm not as free as Alice to do as I please. Although, if you're the only one I tell, I suppose it should be fine.

Fetters.Title: About the Vision
Fetters.Context: This isn't just decorative. It's proof that I've been recognized by the heavens once again — but this time, as a "human." Huh? What does being "human" mean to me? Oh. Well, it's being free and... not so free, I suppose. Come to think of it, perhaps my circumstances haven't improved that much.

Fetters.Title: Something to Share
Fetters.Context: Oh, a story? You really want to hear one? You actually do, don't you? Hehe, that makes me so happy. Most of the time, when I open with, "a long, long time ago" it's because I'm explaining some complicated thing or another. But since we're just chatting... Hm, Arcadia, Lang-Gan, Delphi, Patra... There are so many stories I could tell.

Fetters.Title: Interesting Things
Fetters.Context: Do you know why squirrels are a good metaphor for humans? Because both belong to the, um... "Euarchontoglires" classification. Sure, let's go with that. Are you familiar with that term? I learned it from Alice.

Fetters.Title: About Alice: Encounter
Fetters.Context: Back then, after I sacrificed most of my knowledge, power, and ultimately my voice to maintain a human form, I spent the long, meandering days in listless wandering. Until I met Alice, and she corrupted me.

Fetters.Title: About Alice: Daughter
Fetters.Context: In truth, it's not that I'm any smarter than the other angels. I chose to offer up my voice back then because, well... How does that saying go? "Fortune favors the bold"? The heavens demanded silence and punishment, so I thought a lifetime of voicelessness could be a good bargaining chip. Alice had quite an interesting remark after hearing about it all. She said, "The daughter of the sea offered up her voice to gain legs, the children of the air did the same to gain form, and now the daughter of the heavens sacrificed her voice to become human."

Fetters.Title: About Klee
Fetters.Context: Klee is adorable. But Alice is worried I might have a bad influence on her brain development, so she doesn't let me speak in her head. Do you think that makes me seem weird to Klee? Ask her for me next time, will you? And put in a good word!

Fetters.Title: About Albedo
Fetters.Context: In another converged possibility, Albedo was the "Primordial Human" who traveled through worlds, recording and creating all. However, once that journey reached its end, no one would have been able to guarantee whether he would choose destruction or salvation. Luckily, you're the one who embarked on the journey this time. Hm? How did the alternate Albedo fare? I can no longer observe that possibility. Besides, copying someone else's answer is of little use. This journey is your own.

Fetters.Title: About Mona
Fetters.Context: So, for some strange reason, Mona and Barbie are still refusing to meet face to face, and their only contact is through letters? Well, I suppose it's understandable. It wasn't until after Rhinedottir turned twenty that I actually met her in person. Alice said it wasn't convenient for Khaenri'ahns to come and go, so she brought letters back and forth instead.

Fetters.Title: About Prune
Fetters.Context: Alice said she sent that little Witch Hunter on her quest "because it makes life more entertaining." She warned me to be careful not to get caught, but this little girl sounds like quite an interesting child. If she actually manages to find me, I think it would be fun to go treasure hunting together.

Fetters.Title: About Durin
Fetters.Context: I've been Anya's fan since the beginning. I love all her stories. So, of course, I cannot wait to see how this final, ever-evolving masterpiece of hers is going to unfold — and I will protect it at all costs.

Fetters.Title: About Zibai
Fetters.Context: Um, I'd rather us... not meet, actually. It's not that I dislike her, it's more of a... complex on my part. Kinda like how a weak student feels running into the top of the class. The older angels loved to talk about the exceptional young woman from the neighboring area with ties to the Archangel. Safe to say, Zibai's name came up a lot.

Fetters.Title: About Yae Miko
Fetters.Context: It's such a shame that Yae Publishing House wasn't around during Anya's era of writing. I don't think commercial writing is at odds with Anya's style at all. I'm sure Miss Yae cares about the business side of things, but her personal taste is what drives her success. At the very least, she doesn't confine authors to a box or mass-produce a single trope until something more popular comes along. She focuses on introducing things that are new and fresh.

Fetters.Title: About Fischl
Fetters.Context: Hmph, I'm the one who deserves her admiration. The book was my idea to begin with. All Mr. Nine did was refine it, polish it, and... actually write it all out.

Fetters.Title: More About Nicole: I
Fetters.Context: Making sculptures is my way of keeping records. The written word loses meaning over time. Besides, documents crumble and disintegrate, while paintings return to blank canvases as their pigment fades. Sculptures, at least, have a better chance of standing the test of time... And, no. It's not because my handwriting is ugly, I'm too lazy to write things down, or I can't be bothered to practice painting. Just so we're clear.

Fetters.Title: More About Nicole: II
Fetters.Context: Hm, the idea of writing a novel came about after reading Anya's work. That's when I first discovered that the metaphors angels love so much could be used to tell stories. I had something to say, along with a wealth of indelible memories to draw upon. So, it felt both like something I was meant to do and a path to self-fulfillment all at once. In the end, though, I was far too lazy to follow through.

Fetters.Title: More About Nicole: III
Fetters.Context: Lost knowledge can be relearned over time. Lost power can be regained with training. But, when certain possibilities run their course or fade away, it is best for them to remain forgotten. After all, what matters most is the stability of the present moment. Letting regret or remorse drive us to resurrect what has been lost could leave us with a future we are unable to control.

Fetters.Title: More About Nicole: IV
Fetters.Context: The angels who forsook wisdom in favor of power became corrupted dragons like Ursa, ones without distinct Elemental characteristics. Of course, over time, heroes have hunted them to near extinction. What if I had chosen power like those dragons? ...It's difficult to imagine. I'd like to think my scales would have been a glittering silver, though.

Fetters.Title: More About Nicole: V
Fetters.Context: Humanity is such an intriguing, brilliant species. Giving up my wings was not a fall from grace, but a chance to become part of something tangible. Sacrificing my voice did not silence me, but gave me the strength to listen before judging. Divine potential lies within each and every human, and each day I spend among you is more fascinating than the last.

Fetters.Title: Nicole's Hobbies
Fetters.Context: Does that... not go without saying?

Fetters.Title: Nicole's Troubles
Fetters.Context: At this point, all my closest friends know I'm a chatterbox. So does that mean I don't have to pretend anymore? But what if that gives people headaches, and no one wants to talk to me ever again... Hm, plus Klee's still not old enough. Guess I need to put on the dignified angel act a little longer.

Fetters.Title: Favorite Food
Fetters.Context: I'm not really picky about food. But, I love hot peppers, green onions, and garlic — the stronger the flavor, the better. Not necessarily because of the taste itself, though. When you don't have to open your mouth to speak, you don't have to worry about the spice burning your throat. So, when Alice, Barbie, and I had a spicy food-eating contest, I won. Even with tears streaming down my face, and feeling like my lips were on fire, I won. Sore throat, bad breath... Hehe, doesn't mean a thing to me. Of course, I did brush my teeth and wash out my mouth afterwards.

Fetters.Title: Least Favorite Food
Fetters.Context: Hm... That's a tough question. As long as the food is made well, I'll pretty much eat anything.

Fetters.Title: Receiving a Gift: I
Fetters.Context: Mmm! This is... absolutely delicious!

Fetters.Title: Receiving a Gift: II
Fetters.Context: How sweet. I can certainly tell how much thought you put into it.

Fetters.Title: Receiving a Gift: III
Fetters.Context: Wh—What a... curious aroma. Do I have to?

Fetters.Title: Birthday
Fetters.Context: Happy Birthday! I hid your birthday gift inside a chest and set up a bunch of puzzles for you to solve. Come on, I'll guide you through it!

Fetters.Title: Feelings About Ascension: Intro
Fetters.Context: "Just like that, the group was thrown into a panic, staring at Nicole in utter disbelief until a question broke the silence, 'You knocked Alice out? Why would you do that, Nicole?'"

Fetters.Title: Feelings About Ascension: Building Up
Fetters.Context: "The very picture of calm, Nicole raised a finger and replied, 'Alice has exhausted her strength. She'd be nothing but a burden out here on the battlefield. Now, we can take her to rest.'"

Fetters.Title: Feelings About Ascension: Climax
Fetters.Context: "'But without Alice, how can we possibly hope to be a match for Rhinedottir?' the group wondered, dismissing Nicole's suggestion. Seeing their frowns of doubt, Nicole stepped forward..."

Fetters.Title: Feelings About Ascension: Conclusion
Fetters.Context: "'Before you stands an angel who has wandered this earth for millennia,' she replied. Upon her declaration, the clouds parted, a golden staircase unfolding at her feet, and with a simple step, Nicole began to ascend the stairs of divinity."

Fetters.Title: Special Charged Attack: I
Fetters.Context: Do I sense... treasure?

Fetters.Title: Special Charged Attack: II
Fetters.Context: Where is it...

Fetters.Title: Elemental Skill: I
Fetters.Context: I think not.

Fetters.Title: Elemental Skill: II
Fetters.Context: Begone.

Fetters.Title: Elemental Skill: III
Fetters.Context: Such defiance.

Fetters.Title: Elemental Burst: I
Fetters.Context: Our little secret.

Fetters.Title: Elemental Burst: II
Fetters.Context: I live within you.

Fetters.Title: Elemental Burst: III
Fetters.Context: "Heaven's will, made mine."

Fetters.Title: Opening Treasure Chest: I
Fetters.Context: Oooh! Ahem... Mmm.

Fetters.Title: Opening Treasure Chest: II
Fetters.Context: I'm not sure whose work this was, but this one was hidden quite well.

Fetters.Title: Low HP: I
Fetters.Context: Alice! Barbie! A little help here!

Fetters.Title: Low HP: II
Fetters.Context: Uh oh...

Fetters.Title: Ally at Low HP: I
Fetters.Context: Rest, for I am with you.

Fetters.Title: Fallen: I
Fetters.Context: So much for that... novel...

Fetters.Title: Fallen: II
Fetters.Context: All possibilities... come to a close...

Fetters.Title: Heavy Hit Taken: I
Fetters.Context: Oof...

Fetters.Title: Joining Party: I
Fetters.Context: I shall be your guide.

Fetters.Title: Joining Party: II
Fetters.Context: Mhmhm...

FetterStories.Title: Character Details
FetterStories.Context:
Many of the stories Nicole envisions begin with a phrase as old as the stories themselves: "A long, long time ago..."
It was a time, a long, long, time ago, when the lights of myriad nations filigreed the earth in gleaming gold, when the daughters of heaven wandered in carefree grace betwixt divine courts and mortal cities... It was a time, a long, long, time ago, when the sovereign of ages past, who had descended into deepest darkness, had not yet brought disaster back to his homeland, when the radiant moons that hung aloft in the night heavens were still three...
The years brook no pause, the wheel spinning onward all the same. In a cycle emptied of hope, the spark of paradise perished alongside lives extinguished. Servitors once loyal to their ruler turned traitor for the sake of the lives that had been fashioned; the black dragonlord returned from beyond the stars, and the moonlit chariot of the heavens shattered like crystal...
"Wait a second, that's too much all at once. Just take any one of those threads and spin it a bit more, and you'll have enough for a whole book." The mage writing the story holds her hand up to stop Nicole as she speaks. Her brows knit together as she tries to think. Too many stories are pressing in on her at once, and as each opens into still more thoughts, she finds herself unsure where her pen ought to go next...
"Oh no, that's perfect. Give them a few puzzles to chew on right from the start, and they'll be hooked for sure!" The cheerful mage lay draped over her Dodoco plush as she let out a hearty laugh. Although she has no idea if her friend's work would ever reach a common reader, the mere prospect of a tea party has filled her with enough delight to last a long while.
"Hehe... Who knows, these stories might end up attracting more than just readers. They might even invite certain 'visitors' from higher places." The mage who can read the future chuckles. The veil between the here and now and what is yet to come may have been lifted for her long ago, yet she never speaks of what she sees. After all, the true colors of fate are never meant to be revealed lightly.
"Oh? Well, now you've made things sound even more interesting. I'd quite like to meet these so-called 'visitors.'" The inquisitive mage gives an airy clap before falling silent, as though she is truly looking forward to some uninvited guest's arrival.
"..." The mage who would soon be off again does not say a word. She gives the floor a neat tap with the heel of her thick boot, and the rustling in the suitcase beside her stops right away. Now, she can pick up her tea and enjoy it thoroughly again.
"R-R-Really? Then maybe I should tell a different one instead... one with a little more detail to it?"
The mage without a voice blinks.
Since the tea party won't be ending for a while, she decides to set that story aside and starts telling another instead.
"It was a time, a long, long, time ago..."

FetterStories.Title: Character Story 1
FetterStories.Context:
Nicole was young for an envoy of the heavens, and among the youngest, in fact.
Granted the holy favor of the heavens like her sisters, she was endowed with both wisdom and sacred authority and called to fulfill that supreme will.
The elder angels spoke, and told her this:
Once upon a time, the angels had kindled light in their palms and taught mortals the uses of that flame: to drive back the night, to turn seed and flesh into sustenance, and to ward off the beasts baring their fangs. They had twisted hemp into rope and taught the mortals to use it to scale down the cliffs, to weave garments and shoes, and to fashion vessels fit to cross the rivers... They had spoken, too, of thoughts profound and of ideals pure, teaching the humans to steady their hearts, to pass through hardship and peril with fortitude and diligence, and to dream dreams that belonged to mankind alone.
And following that...
The elder angels spoke again, and told her this:
There was a city that had taken fire and bound it to their rule, ensuring the light within their walls would never dim. Yet, that which could have sustained them and given them prosperity was turned to destruction, for they chose to unleash that very light upon their own people. Bright as the sun the fire was, and it reduced countless innocent lives to bone and ash. The earth that bore its passing was left level as a mirror, stripped of every sign of life. And in the aftermath, countless more were cast out to wander. At last, the high heavens extinguished this flame, furrowed the dead earth where no life remained, and by the lives of the punished awoke life there anew. Elsewhere, there was a city that wove all things from rope, yet sought to climb to the most sacred heavens by the golden cords in their hands. They painted countless golden strands upon canvas, drew them forth, and bound them into an upside-down tower rising toward the heavens, seeking to reach the supreme fruit they had imagined there. Eventually, the high heavens severed those cords of arrogance and sealed the trespassers within the canvas. There, the golden cords they had woven bound them for all eternity, and those beyond the canvas were brought low to their knees, never again to turn their faces upward to the heavens. And then there was another city that pushed the wisdom of the world to its farthest reach. To attain the ultimate understanding, they cast aside the bodies they saw as burdens and descended, one by one, into the lightless depths of the sea. Upon the waters there remained only thin shells adrift, and through his eyes and hands they continued their exploration of the world. Yet, before his sight could be set upon the dark gulf beyond this world, the high heavens turned the vast waters of the sea to vapor, purged away the intellect that had led them into delirium, and returned them to their first shape, thereby ending the fevered reveries of that deep transgression.
"The laws shall not be tested, nor shall the strictures be questioned, or the scriptures be infringed."
Thus spoke the eldest sister, once closest of them all to the Lord of the Sky.
"Any transgression shall be greeted by inevitable punishment."
This was an edict that Nicole, too, once believed with all her heart.

FetterStories.Title: Character Story 2
FetterStories.Context:
"Gracious envoy, I have come with another question."
Another day had dawned. A girl came to the temple high above the satellite city and, once again, knocked on its doors.
Inside an inner hall. Nicole, sitting poised on a rug, invited the girl to speak with a smile. The girl answered with a grin and, almost without pausing for breath, began to spill out every single thought she had had from the night before. Her words came and went as swiftly as birds sweeping low over water, but Nicole caught them all. But of course she did, since she was an angel after all.
The girl was the most curious creature of them all in the city, and it was as if the world itself were a giant unsolved puzzle to her. From the moment Nicole had been sent to keep watch over this place, it was she who came with the most questions, and the oddest ones, too.
There were fools in the city who feared that, sooner or later, the girl would ask a question that strayed too far, and by it call calamity upon them. And so they shut her in a room and bound her in irons, in the hope that hunger and fear might smother the unruly stirrings of her mind.
Then, on the fifth day, when the fools opened the door to her "cell," they found the girl crouched on the ground, carving one complex equation after another into the floor with her shackles. When they stepped into the room at last, wary and unsure, what met them was not the silence of defeat but a sharp reprimand.
"LEAVE MY EQUATIONS ALONE!"
Perhaps the fools were alarmed at the burning intelligence they saw in her eyes. Or maybe they had finally remembered the respect owed to the wise. Whatever the reason, they then gingerly removed her shackles and brought her before Nicole.
Nicole was actually rather fond of the girl's endless questions, for it gave her something she had rarely been afforded before: a chance to truly guide someone. Nicole seldom found much room to put her own gifts to use — as one of the youngest of the angels, she had come only after so many others had already enlightened the people of the world. The people on earth at this point were so remarkably clever that they even perplexed the eldest sister on occasion.
But now, fortune finally had handed her one precious opportunity.
"Gracious envoy, why is it that the fruit upon the trees is always drawn downward to the earth?"
"Why does the thunder always arrive only after the lightning has already flashed?"
"And what keeps the moon from flying far away, so that it rises and sinks before our eyes instead?"
Ah, so what we're really talking about here are gravity and the speed of light versus sound... That's fine, that's totally fine. As long as she doesn't ask me to do any calculations by hand, or start deriving formulas on the spot, I can absolutely handle that. For now, anyway, I can still get away with it just by explaining the basic concepts and principles. That part's easy. Completely manageable. But... isn't this child learning a little too fast? At this rate, in a few more days she's going to start asking me about fluid dynamics, orbital mechanics, thermodynamics, that sort of thing! I can't possibly explain that! Please, no. And besides, this really isn't normal, is it? I'm sure my older sister once told me that the people of the city of Lang-Gan were still learning how to weave fabric... Um... no, no, wait, their way of learning over there was strange too, wasn't it? One group would finish learning weaving, and then right away another group would come in to learn mathematics and measurement. Everyone studied different things, separately. Is that what makes it faster? By having more folks learn different things? Hm. It's impressive that the angel there could keep up at all. Did my elder sister ever tell me her name? What was it again...? I think she was—— Wait, no, no, that is not the point right now! I need to focus, focus! Let's deal with the problem on hand first. Then tonight, I'll do some reading to stay ahead. Yes. Perfect. That's the plan.
So, the angel on the rug felt quite pleased with herself as the girl looked at her expectantly. She gave two little coughs, shifted her body a little, and began explaining once more.
"Ahem. So, the answer to your question is..."

FetterStories.Title: Character Story 3
FetterStories.Context:
"Gracious envoy..."
The clever girl sat on her heels before Nicole, whose face had been left untouched by time. A descendant of that very first sage, she had embarked on the selfsame well-worn path as her predecessors, asked countless questions along the way, and has long earned the city's respect.
Outside the window, flying ships zipped by, guided by hands well-versed in the language of the wind. On board these ships, men and women dressed in silk spoke freely of philosophy and literature, and in their rooms, the lamps burned on placidly and with no end.
But carefree as the city was, that same ease was never quite felt by what was the brightest mind among them all. Instead, she remained troubled by questions no sage before her had ever thought to raise.
She appeared once more before that angel who had answered countless questions of hers and those before, and humbly posed that question that vexed her so.
"Please tell me... what purpose does this world serve?"
Nicole, angel as she was, found herself stumped by this question. Despite the wisdom granted to her by the heavens, she did not have an answer to that...
"Purpose" implied an ending, and all things made by the supreme power, by that very measure, were bound one day to reach their end and extinction. Nothing in all creation was meant to remain forever.
But her eldest sister had said that this world, too, had been made by that same supreme power. Would that mean, then... that the world itself would also be brought to nothing?
But...
Nicole's thoughts halted abruptly at this point. But this was not the awkward pause of someone at a loss for words.
Rather, for reasons beyond her knowing, the grace of the heavens no longer responded to her call.
The angel who had been dwelling here in solitude was plunged into confusion. Her world had never been so deathly silent, and the hollow stillness left her at a loss.
Later on, she glimpsed something through an open doorway: between heaven and earth, a crimson rain of fire and feathers was falling without end. The giant spike of judgment hammered the ground, and with every booming strike, another city was erased and silenced.
"...Go. Now."
"Revered—..."
The angel sprang to her feet and shook the girl as she yelled,
"Gather everyone and follow me! Hurry!"
Though she no longer had the grace of the heavens, she could still do this one thing: gather the people and lead them to safety.
And thus began a long retreat fraught with confusion and uncertainty. Having lost everything, the people were effectively set adrift. They had long since forgotten how to survive by the labor of their own hands, and all their fine silks could not ward off the wind, the rain, or the bitter cold of the wilds.
"It's fine, I'll teach you. First, you whittle the wood down like this, then you hold it over the fire, and then... See? It's kindled! No, no, not like that, you have to..."
"Start with the stone, you have to sharpen the stone first. Yes, just like that... See? Now you have a simple knife."
"And then, ah... right, the shelter... We'll have to hammer those stakes in first, so I'll need a few strong folks to help me. What? No one? Fine, we'll just need more hands on this then!"
Guess I'll just have to guide them for a longer while, Nicole thought. She was a very capable angel, after all, just like her elder sisters. She could teach these little ones everything once again: how to survive and which trees bore fruit, how to plant seed-bearing greens, and how to hunt the birds, beasts, and fish. She would show them how to knot cords to mark their days, teach them that one and one made two, and show them how, one day, they might build palaces and temples again...
But this was no longer a world indulgent enough to let her — or them — start from the very beginning again.
In the world she had known, food and rain would descend from the heavens as an answer to famine, while poverty was invariably salved by the ore, gold, and jade yielded by the earth.
Yet neither that sovereign on high, nor the sister who ruled the nations in their place, had ever told her what to do when the cold soil was already being choked by a pale calamity that draped over the land like a burial shroud. How was she to sow life in earth like that with nothing but wild seed? And if plague and hunger should threaten these puny lives, how was she to summon from nothing a remedy for every ill?
The brilliant angel had already labored to master every theory and field of knowledge that the age held as the key to happiness. Yet, when applied to the art of being happy, theories were only theories, and knowledge was nothing more than a pale approximation.
...
The last to fall asleep was always that cleverest girl of them all.
"Gracious envoy..."
The girl asked, as she lay in Nicole's arms,
"Please tell me... just what purpose does this world serve?"
"..."
"I don't know,"
said Nicole.
No answer could have sounded less fitting from one such as her.
But she really did not know the answer.

FetterStories.Title: Character Story 4
FetterStories.Context:
The heavens burned until there was nothing left to fight over.
The broken feathers torn loose during the rebellion rained down alongside cursed blood and shattered bone, as both the abyss's baleful shadow and the heavens were consumed by fire.
But the seed of defiance had long since been planted deep in the souls of heaven's envoys. They had seen the winged ones in the sky sing freely and pour out their true loves and hates without restraint. Fleeting dream as it was, how could one pretend that such blazing passion does not exist after that? How could one go back to singing hymns for the tyrant above, and willingly take the yoke upon their neck again?
And so, the angels, once graced with what was rightly theirs, had to bear the punishment their sins had earned.
Those who refused to relinquish their powers were stripped of their wisdom and turned into filthy beasts which would then spend their days shrinking away from the eyes of judgment. Others were stripped of everything but a lone remnant of will. They would spread their golden wings, then dive deep into the sea where they would ride the tides until the hour they would play their fated theme. Some would exhaust every effort to hide their forms in the restless, shifting yellow sands, though they were unable to outrun the eternal chase of judgment nevertheless. And then there were those who would look with melancholy upon the shackles the heavens had cast over the world, even as they poured themselves into securing just a fragile spark of life for the sheltered seedlings amidst the cataclysm of the mortal plane. In the end, they would find themselves drifting eternally through that frozen instant when even the ever-present wind had fallen silent. Others would meld into the earth, and under the silent gaze of the supreme, they would breathe life into the barren red sands, and cradle the first trembling sprouts of a new beginning...
Then, at last, it was Nicole's turn.
Half her wisdom she surrendered, for she knew that it had already been forged by the heavens into a set of shackles. The supreme held all things captive to its will, switching between benevolence and punishment seemingly at whim. Not even the heavenly envoys could shake its core — and she herself had already witnessed the torment these blind shackles had inflicted upon the innocent.
If wisdom required her to turn a blind eye, she would rather leap wordlessly into the mire, dark as it appeared, to pursue the answers the world held for her.
Half her power she surrendered unflinchingly, for she did not fear the thought of taking on mortality like countless others. When the heavens withdrew their grace, she traipsed the barren plains alongside the vast exodus of humanity. She saw their blood and their tears and heard their desperate cries and pleas, sights and sounds that had never reached her when she was sitting on that rug in that peaceful courtyard.
If power required her to turn a deaf ear, she would rather shed the wings that had never truly been hers and set out alone to find the reason for her existence.
And so, Nicole made her way back alone to the silent earth.
But the once-so-mighty angel still could not find the answer to the question of the world's true purpose.

FetterStories.Title: Character Story 5
FetterStories.Context:
"The purpose of the world? Well... But before we go any further, it might be best to first stop ascribing human thoughts and emotions to it..." Mage "M" blinked at the strange question as she prodded at her hair with the feathered end of her quill. A resigned smile appeared on her face.
"To resist destruction," said "O," who was enjoying a rare moment of peace and quiet. She kept her gaze low, letting the silence linger a while before finally adding, "...But in the end, it all comes out the same."
"I have no idea what you're talking about, and I don't feel like figuring it out either," "B" yawned. This was in the days before she met her apprentice with the large hat. "If the world's purpose isn't mine... what, am I supposed to flee from this world then? By the way, where's Alice? No wonder it feels so quiet around here. Is she not here yet?"
"If leaving this world were possible, that would be great," said "R" flippantly. Her eyes were fixed on the flask in her hands, where "life" was quietly taking shape. Then, she set it down and clapped her hands. "Still, that's a pretty interesting question. Could make a nice little test out of it."
As the moon traced another slow circle above, the mages' tea party continued, drifting through casual and sometimes scattered conversation. "M" closed her book and left her seat. "R" and "B" left the party together. Finally, "O" nodded to Nicole and walked out with her rolling suitcase behind her.
Nicole let out a quiet sigh. Even those as powerful as these mages...
"Hey, Nicole! You ready yet?"
"A," who wasn't at the tea party earlier, suddenly appeared behind Nicole and caught her by the shoulder. The hat on "A's" head jingled with every move, and the towering suitcase behind her, nearly twice her height, was also capped with a giant pointy hat.
"Alice? What... are you doing? And what's with that enormous suitcase?"
"Weren't you just asking about the purpose of the world?" The cheerful mage in crimson laughed spiritedly, then said with absolute certainty, "Not that I have any idea!"
"But we can go take a look at other 'worlds.' Don't worry, we'll stay on this side of the border. It's just a little excursion to pass the time while we check out some floating oddities and seek out some inspiration."
"Ah, I'd love nothing more than to take you on a real visit to other worlds just like Octavia. But the border rules are strict, so strict."
"When you come back across the border, you have to leave behind everything that doesn't belong here, except for memories. Though sometimes, even the memories aren't allowed either."
"I mean, imagine coming back and not being able to tell anyone about the talking clouds, the bugs that hide in sounds, the light-eating dust, or those eight-sided active pillars that can't ever catch sight of a cross — all that stuff you saw on the other side!"
"But don't let that get you down. The next time Octavia comes back, we'll make her take us along. And if that doesn't work, we'll stow ourselves away in her suitcase!"
"For now... let's go uncover ourselves a little 'surprise,' shall we?"
And so, the mighty angel and the formidable mage started planning... a little journey. It was not going to be a very long adventure, and besides, Nicole won't be alone this time. The biggest challenge of the trip would probably be just making it through the non-stop string of desserts Alice would be pulling out of thin air.
What is the true purpose of this world?
The angel, shackled by the heavens as she was, could never get an answer to this question.
In that case, it was time for the free-spirited mage to seek out the answer instead...

FetterStories.Title: "Border Pass"
FetterStories.Context:
Alice might have assured her that there would be no trouble and that they weren't going anywhere too far, but arriving at the "border" still meant going through the motions. Forms had to be filled, and every required step followed. The only concession in this whole tedious affair was the two chairs they had to sit on.
Nicole had to spend nearly an entire day filling out form after form before the thick little booklet known as the Border Pass became hers. The moment it landed in her hands, a warning followed: the book must remain absolutely immaculate — one oily spot or fingerprint on its pages, and she would have to apply for a new pass. And should she try to cross the border with a pass that is in less-than-perfect condition, she would be detained for all sorts of checks until the powers-that-be were satisfied.
Interestingly, the very substance of the pass seemed to change depending on the person holding it. Nicole had already seen them come in stone, wood, liquid, vapor (a kind of non-chemical information carrier), and even light forms (de-energized Phlogiston).
"It must be really difficult to keep the border secure," Nicole reflected, as she watched someone carrying an entire stack of clay tablet-type passes hurry by.
"Yeah, and that's why I try not to stay here any longer than necessary," Alice replied as she stepped over a rather wide stream of mercury. In turn, the flowing mercury spun once in a circle, as if to politely acknowledge her passage.
"The paperwork is the worst. I'd much rather be running around outside—— Hey, Kleiner! Over here!"
Alice sprang to her feet and waved vigorously. The figure in the distance, who was juggling eight paper bags, waved back with equal enthusiasm.
"Alright, alright, the goodies are here! Let's eat first, then we can go check out all the other fun things!"

FetterStories.Title: Vision
FetterStories.Context:
"Gods" were something people always needed, for there exists a universal hunger for prayers to be heard, and a desperate longing for someone else to bear the weight of the world.
After mankind lost the angels, the land was left in the hands of strange-looking beings known as the Archons. One wave of the hand by these beings, and those bereft of hope gathered. One cursory promise, and their followers rejoiced. One mere trace of a miracle, and the humans bowed in gratitude, hearts brimming with awe.
"This really isn't a great time to be born human,"
Nicole thought as she stood apart from the crowd. Her sisters had always possessed a serene, sacred beauty — surely, these odd Archons were no match for them? Though admittedly, a few of them could actually be described as "easy on the eyes"...
Nicole's gaze fell on a sister of hers who was now circling her legs in her Seelie form, and she could not help but let out a silent sigh. Her metamorphosed sister seemed intent on dragging her toward a treasure chest, likely because Nicole's current form was so human.
Nicole watched her reflection in the water as she shifted her expression again and again. But no matter how much she tried to summon the divine, not even a trace flickered across her face.
The angels had faded from this world, though mankind would always need its gods.

A long time later. Nicole, who was attuned to every subtle tremor in the world, had felt a faint shift in its rules.
The mark known as the "Vision," a great journey meant for humans alone, stirred something deep inside her. She understood its workings, and she knew the inevitable ending it would lead to. Yet, she wondered whether her near-human state might allow her a fleeting glimpse. As for that final outcome, it was of little import, for consciousness born of light — even when fallen to the mortal realm — invariably strives to return to its origin.
Nicole tried everything to no avail. Thousands of years of emotions were pent up within her, but had nowhere to go. The heavens simply remained indifferently mute.
In great disappointment, she wandered to Natlan, finding there the only family still capable of sharing wisdom through language.
"You'll only be ignored, since they've erased the angels from the world's design and order."
"I get it now. It means I'll need to use my brain and worm my way into the human world, huh?"
Where connections did not exist, she made them; and where she wasn't part of the plan, she made sure she became part of the blueprint nevertheless, one way or another.
Nicole no longer watched from afar. She took on sculpture commissions, the rewards tucked into treasure chests. She guided the lost in silence, handing them the chests before she sent them off on the right path. She quietly offered clues to scholars adrift in a sea of confusion...

Slowly, almost imperceptibly, the results of her actions gathered, everything fell naturally into place, and Nicole's Vision arrived without fanfare. What tipped the balance was the simple act of helping an old man, a quiet moment where her impact could no longer be ignored by the sky.
Mankind will always need its gods. This is a fact that applies to angels too. To be ignored, to be no longer needed, is a fate that terrifies even the divine.
With her Vision pinched between her thumb and index finger, Nicole held it up to the sky until it eclipsed the outline of Celestia.
O, my Creator...
"...Finally, you see me as human. You must forgive me and protect me. From this day on, I shall live a little more freely and boldly."

Costumes.Name: Mimetic Interpretation
Costumes.Description: Nicole's outfit. "This is the winged raiment, woven from prose and poetry, of an angel who has wandered the earth for thousands of years..." Just kidding, of course. If the above is true, why would this outfit not change every day? And to take a step back, this fantasy should not be further explored, lest one's tea parties turn into costume balls. And in any case, it's not as if the one in the heavens forbade people from dressing up as angels.

NameCard.Name: Nicole: Heavenly Stairway
NameCard.Description:
Namecard style.
Once, Nicole still bore a form close to that of a human, making her way down the stairway step by step. Whatever else it was, it seemed far more dignified than falling or being cast down. Silent all the while, she passed beneath the sky and into a city that no longer belonged to angels.
